title: Alamar Blue Assay: Drug Sensitivity Test
steps: Take 96 well plate and add 200ml of 1X sterile PBS in all the outer wells like starting from A -H- 12.
Count your cells and adjust it according to desired concentration like for Diplonema papillatum -2x10 5 cells/ml
Add 100ul of media in all the wells

Add 16ul of drug and 184ul of media in the first well and mix it properly and dilute the drug across the plate by transfer 100ul of media containing antibiotics to rest of the wells and discard the last 100ul
Add 200ul of cells to each well starting from last well with lowest antibiotic concentration and now a final concentration of cells are 1x10 5 cells/ml.
Incubate the cells in the wet chamber for 2 days at 27°C.
Observe the cells every day to check whether they are happy or dying.
Add 40ul of Alamar blue assay solution or Resazurin cells by dissolving in 1.25mg/ml 1X PBS, pH should be 7.4, when cells look like they are dying
Incubate them for 17h for minimum and 24h maximum.
Read the fluorescence by using a fluorescence excitation wavelength of 540-570nm.
Analyze the data using GraphPad Prism software using nonlinear regression and sigmoidal dose -response analysis
creator: Binnypreet Kaur
